{{Quote|My n-name is Marrt. ''Sergeant'' Marrt of the ''182nd Legion''.|[[Marrt]]|<ref name="Tale" />}}
The '''182nd Legion''' was a [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone trooper]] [[Legion/Legends|unit]] in the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], a [[Civil war|conflict]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]]. In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], a [[Squad/Legends|squad]] of the Legion was deployed on a [[Scout/Legends|reconnaissance]] [[Mission to Belgaroth|mission]] that resulted in the [[Death/Legends|deaths]] of three [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]]—[[Chatter]], [[Knuckles (182nd Legion)|Knuckles]] and [[Recon]]; only their commanding officer, [[Clone Sergeant/Legends|Sergeant]] [[Marrt]], survived and completed the mission. During the final [[Standard year/Legends|year]] of the war, the 182nd Legion was [[Battle of Felucia (Outer Rim Sieges)/Legends|deployed]] to the Separatist [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Felucia/Legends|Felucia]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]] and attempted to retake the planet for the Republic. They soon lost communication with their superiors, however, and the task fell to a contingent of the [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]]. The 501st was later rotated out of Felucia, but not before they discovered that the 182nd had been destroyed.

==History==
The 182nd [[Legion/Legends|Legion]] was a [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone trooper]] unit in the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]],<ref name="Tale" /> a [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic-level]] [[Civil war|conflict]] which began as a [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|major secessionist movement]] that resulted in the formation of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]].<ref name="Episode II">{{Film|II}}</ref> In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], rumors began to spread about a Republic-affiliated [[Czerka factory|factory]] operated by [[Czerka Arms/Legends|Czerka Corporation]] was secretly supplying weapons to the Confederacy. In response, [[Clone Sergeant/Legends|Clone Sergeant]] [[Marrt]] and a [[Squad/Legends|squad]] of [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]]—[[Chatter]], [[Knuckles (182nd Legion)|Knuckles]] and [[Recon]]—from the 182nd were dispatched to [[Belgaroth]] with orders to investigate the allegations of Czerka duplicity.<ref name="Tale" />

[[File:Marrt_in_medical_facility.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Marrt, sole survivor of a squad in the 182nd Legion]]
The squad confirmed Czerka's treachery when [[Sergeant/Legends|Sergeant]] Marrt witnessed [[Tarin (Human)|Tarin]], the head of the factory, in the process of facilitating a shipping arrangement of [[FEX-M3 nerve gas grenade]]s with [[Reep Tonith]] of the [[InterGalactic Banking Clan/Legends|InterGalactic Banking Clan]], one of several powerful companies aligned with the Confederacy. The [[Grenade/Legends|grenades]] had been declared illegal by the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]] a [[Standard year/Legends|year]] earlier, but this did not prevent Tarin from agreeing to sell them to [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]],<ref name="Tale" /> the [[Head of State/Legends|leader]] of the Separatist rebellion.<ref name="Episode II" /> The [[Cloning/Legends|clones]] were forced to retreat upon being discovered by a [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-series battle droid]], although Marrt managed to retrieve one of the grenades as evidence for exposing Czerka's illegal activities. After falling back to a swamp, the four troopers were attacked by a hostile creature known as a [[droidbreaker]] and resulted in the [[Death/Legends|deaths]] of Recon, Chatter and Knuckles; only Marrt survived and killed the beast with his stolen FEX-M3 grenade.<ref name="Tale" />

With the loss of his squadmates and the evidence, Marrt resolved to complete the [[Mission to Belgaroth]] on his own. He returned to the factory, fought against the Banking Clan's [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] for several [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]] and ultimately burned the facility to the ground shortly after obtaining Czerka's data records as proof of the company's dealings with the enemies of the Republic. Though heavily wounded, he escaped from Belgaroth and was [[Transport/Legends|transported]] to a [[Sacorria clone medical and rehabilitation facility|Republic medical facility]] on [[Sacorria]] where he was able to personally deliver the evidence to [[High Jedi General/Legends|High Jedi General]] [[Kit Fisto/Legends|Kit Fisto]] and [[Jedi Commander/Legends|Jedi Commander]] [[Ahsoka Tano/Legends|Ahsoka Tano]].<ref name="Tale" />

In [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], the last year of the Clone Wars, the 182nd Legion was deployed to the Separatist-held world of [[Felucia/Legends|Felucia]], one of several key targets in the Republic's [[Outer Rim Sieges/Legends|campaign]] to reconquer the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. The Legion successfully landed on the planet, but were soon cut off from communications with the Republic. Isolated and unheard from for a [[Standard month/Legends|month]], reinforcements from the [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]] were sent to fulfill the 182nd's objectives. Yet the 501st also endured many hardships in navigating through a hazardous terrain filled with hostile [[Acklay/Legends|Acklays]], flesh-eating bacteria and many other obstacles that made Felucia extremely difficult to subjugate. Under the leadership of [[Jedi General/Legends|Jedi General]] [[Aayla Secura/Legends|Aayla Secura]], the 501st ultimately survived their ordeal on Felucia but discovered that the 182nd had been wiped out.<ref name="Battlefront II" />

==Behind the scenes==
The 182nd Legion was first mentioned in the ''[[Star Wars: Battlefront II]]'', a [[Star Wars video games|video game]] released in [[2005]] and the sequel to ''[[Star Wars: Battlefront]]''. Sergeant Marrt and his squad first appeared in ''[[A Trooper's Tale]]'', a short [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] strip published in [[2010]] as part of ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars Comic 6.8|Star Wars: The Clone Wars Comic UK 6.8]]''.
